coreConcept: The Water Cycle is like Earth's giant recycling machine for water! It's how water moves all around our planet, from the sky to the ground and back again, making sure we always have water to drink and for plants to grow.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun is like the big engine that powers the whole water cycle, giving energy to turn liquid water into invisible gas!

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Oceans, Lakes, & Rivers
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Major sources of water for evaporation and collection points.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: These are the giant bathtubs and flowing rivers where most of Earth's water lives, waiting to start its journey into the sky.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Form when water vapor condenses and hold water before precipitation.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds are like fluffy sky-sponges made of tiny water droplets or ice crystals, holding water high above us until it's ready to fall.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Release water vapor into the air through transpiration.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Plants are amazing! They drink water from the ground and then 'sweat' some of it out as invisible water vapor into the air, helping the cycle along.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Gravity
keyActorsAndComponents.role: Pulls water down as precipitation and causes runoff.
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Gravity is the invisible force that pulls everything down to Earth, making rain fall from clouds and rivers flow downhill.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation & Transpiration
storyOfProcess.description: The Sun warms up water in oceans, lakes, and rivers, turning it into an invisible gas called water vapor that floats up into the sky. Plants also release water vapor!

storyOfProcess.name: Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: High up in the sky, the water vapor gets cold and turns back into tiny liquid water droplets or ice crystals. These tiny bits join together to form clouds!

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When clouds get too full of water, the water falls back to Earth as rain, snow, sleet, or hail. This is called precipitation!

storyOfProcess.name: Collection & Runoff
storyOfProcess.description: The fallen water collects in oceans, lakes, and rivers, or soaks into the ground. Some of it flows over the land as runoff, making its way back to larger bodies of water, ready to start the cycle again!

corePrinciples.name: Water is Always Moving
corePrinciples.description: The water on Earth is never still; it's always on an amazing journey, changing places and forms.

corePrinciples.name: Water Changes States
corePrinciples.description: Water can be a liquid (like in a river), a gas (like invisible water vapor), or a solid (like ice or snow), and it changes between these forms in the cycle.

corePrinciples.name: Water is Recycled
corePrinciples.description: The water cycle is Earth's way of cleaning and reusing water over and over again, so we never run out!
